---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
#library(pracma)
#library(peakPick)
#library(quantmod)
library(ggpmisc)
library(tidyverse)
library(magrittr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggfortify)
library(forecast)
library(tsibble)
library(broom)
library(tidyquant)
library(tsbox)
#library("pheatmap")
library("RColorBrewer")
library(ggpmisc)
library(ggfortify)
library(viridis) # Default Color Maps from 'matplotlib' 
library(hrbrthemes)
library(data.table)
#library(superheat)
```

## load existing/saved environment (.RData)
```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "df_list.RData",  sep = "/")
load(file=saved_path)
```

## Read .csv files
```{r}
subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
Kymo_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Kymo_", subfolder_list) ]
```

```{r}
pat_list <- list(Tip_actin="^Actin_tip(.*)csv$",  Tip_Kif5C= "^Kif5C_tip(.*)csv$", Neurite_actin= "^Actin_neurite(.*)csv$", Neurite_Kif5C= "^Kif5C_neurite(.*)csv$")
```

# Neurite length
```{r}

subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
Kymo_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Kymo_", subfolder_list) ]

length_list<- list()
freq <- 1 # set frequency (1/interval)
pixel <- 0.45 # set pixel size

# read csv files in the subfolders of subfolder list
for (n in 1:length(Kymo_list))  
{
  csv_list <- list.files(Kymo_list[n],pattern = "^Actin_tip(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  df <- do.call("cbind", lapply(csv_list, read.csv, header = TRUE))
  df <-df[, grep("Tip.point",  colnames(df)), drop = FALSE] %>% as.data.table()
  df <- mapply(`*`,df, pixel)
  df <-pracma::hampel(df, k=5)$y # remove jumping spikes
  colnames(df)<- paste("Length", seq( from = 1, to = dim(df)[2] ),  sep = ".")
  
  length_list[[n]] <-df

}

names(length_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)

```

# Kif5C intensity at neurite tip (30 pixels)
```{r}

subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
Kymo_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Kymo_", subfolder_list) ]

Tip_Kif5C_list<- list()

# read csv files in the subfolders of subfolder list
for (n in 1:length(Kymo_list))  
{
  csv_list <- list.files(Kymo_list[n],pattern = "^Kif5C_tip(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  df <- do.call("cbind", lapply(csv_list, read.csv, header = TRUE))
  
  df <-df[, grep("Integerated.intensity",  colnames(df)), drop = FALSE] %>% as.data.table() 
  df <-mapply(`*`,df, 1/10000)
  df <-pracma::hampel(df, k=5)$y # remove jumping spikes
  
  colnames(df)<- paste("Tip_KIF5C", seq( from = 1, to = ncol(df) ),  sep = ".")
  Tip_Kif5C_list[[n]]<-  df

}

names(Tip_Kif5C_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)
```

# Kif5C intensity at neurite shaft (whole neurite length, including tip)
```{r}
subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
Kymo_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Kymo_", subfolder_list) ]

Neurite_Kif5C_list<- list()

# read csv files in the subfolders of subfolder list
for (n in 1:length(Kymo_list))  
{
  csv_list <- list.files(Kymo_list[n],pattern = "^Kif5C_neurite(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  df <- do.call("cbind", lapply(csv_list, read.csv, header = TRUE))
  
  df <-df[, grep("Integerated.intensity",  colnames(df)), drop = FALSE] %>% as.data.table()
  df <- mapply(`*`,df, 1/10000)
  df <-pracma::hampel(df, k=5)$y # remove jumping spikes
  colnames(df)<- paste("Neurite_Kif5C", seq( from = 1, to = dim(df)[2] ),  sep = ".")
  
  Neurite_Kif5C_list[[n]]<-df
}

names(Neurite_Kif5C_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)
```

# Actin intensity at neurite tip (30 pixels)
```{r}
subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
Kymo_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Kymo_", subfolder_list) ]

Tip_actin_list<- list()

# read csv files in the subfolders of subfolder list
for (n in 1:length(Kymo_list))  
{
  csv_list <- list.files(Kymo_list[n],pattern = "^Actin_tip(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  df <- do.call("cbind", lapply(csv_list, read.csv, header = TRUE))
  
  df <-df[, grep("Integerated.intensity",  colnames(df)), drop = FALSE] %>% as.data.table() 
  df <-mapply(`*`,df, 1/10000)
  df <-pracma::hampel(df, k=5)$y # remove jumping spikes
  
  colnames(df)<- paste("Tip_actin", seq( from = 1, to = ncol(df) ),  sep = ".")
  Tip_actin_list[[n]]<-  df

}

names(Tip_actin_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)
```

# Actin intensity at neurite shaft (whole neurite length, including tip)
```{r}
subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
Kymo_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Kymo_", subfolder_list) ]

Neurite_actin_list<- list()

# read csv files in the subfolders of subfolder list
for (n in 1:length(Kymo_list))  
{
  csv_list <- list.files(Kymo_list[n],pattern = "^Actin_neurite(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  df <- do.call("cbind", lapply(csv_list, read.csv, header = TRUE))
  
  df <-df[, grep("Integerated.intensity",  colnames(df)), drop = FALSE] %>% as.data.table()
  df <- mapply(`*`,df, 1/10000)
  df <-pracma::hampel(df, k=5)$y # remove jumping spikes
  colnames(df)<- paste("Neurite_actin", seq( from = 1, to = dim(df)[2] ),  sep = ".")
  
  Neurite_actin_list[[n]]<-df
}

names(Neurite_actin_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)
```

# Actin and Kif5C density of the whole neurite ((tip + neurite shaft)/neurite length))
```{r}

Density_actin_list<- list()
Density_Kif5C_list<- list()

for (n in 1:length(length_list))  {

  df <- (Neurite_actin_list[[n]])/(length_list[[n]])
  colnames(df)<- paste("density_actin", seq( from = 1, to = dim(df)[2] ),  sep = ".")
  df[sapply(df, is.infinite)] <- NA
  Density_actin_list[[n]]<- df
  
  df <- (Neurite_Kif5C_list[[n]])/(length_list[[n]])
  colnames(df)<- paste("density_Kif5C", seq( from = 1, to = dim(df)[2] ),  sep = ".")
  df[sapply(df, is.infinite)] <- NA
  Density_Kif5C_list[[n]]<- df
  
}

names(Density_actin_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)
names(Density_Kif5C_list) <- Kymo_list %>% basename() %>% gsub("Kymo_", "", .)
```

```{r}
all_list <- list(length = length_list, 
                 tip_actin = Tip_actin_list,  
                 tip_Kif5C = Tip_Kif5C_list, 
                 neurite_actin = Neurite_actin_list, 
                 neurite_Kif5C = Neurite_Kif5C_list, 
                 Density_actin = Density_actin_list, 
                 Density_Kif5C = Density_Kif5C_list)
```

# Create stationary (differential) curve of all measurements
```{r}
all_sta_list <- list()

freq <- 1

for (m in 1:length(all_list)) {
  
  selected_list <-all_list[[m]]
  
  sta_list <- list()

  for (n in 1:length(selected_list))  {
 
  sta_list[[n]]<- selected_list[[n]] %>% 
    forecast::ma(5) %>%                                    # smoothing #1
    MTS::diffM(d=1) %>% 
    forecast::ma(5) %>% 
    ts(frequency = freq, start = c(0, 1))
  
  colnames(sta_list[[n]])<-colnames(selected_list[[n]])
  
  }

names(sta_list)<- names(selected_list)
  
all_sta_list[[m]]<- sta_list
  
}

names(all_sta_list)<- names(all_list )

```

# Prepare data for cross-correlation analysis at individual neurite level
```{r}
library("data.table")

all_sta_cell_list <- list()

for (k in 1:length(length_list)){
  
neurite_list <- list()

dt1 <- all_sta_list[[1]][[k]] %>% as.data.table()
NC <- dt1 %>% ncol()

    for (n in 1:NC){
      
     dt <- dt1[,..n]
     
          for (m in 2:7){
            dt2 <- all_sta_list[[m]][[k]] %>% as.data.table()
            dt<- cbind(dt, dt2[, ..n])
          }
    neurite_list[[n]] <- dt
    }
names(neurite_list)<- paste("Neurite_", 1:NC, sep = "")
all_sta_cell_list[[k]]<- neurite_list
}

names(all_sta_cell_list) <- names(all_sta_list[[1]])
```

# cross-correlation with neurite length at individual neurite level
```{r warning=FALSE}
ccf_neurite_length_list <- list()
selected_list <- all_sta_cell_list
selected_list <- unlist(selected_list, recursive=FALSE)

for (n in 2:7){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (selected_list[[1]][ ,1],  
                      selected_list[[1]][ ,..n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,
                            plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (selected_list[[m]][ ,1],  
                      selected_list[[m]][ ,..n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,
                            plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
  
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_neurite_length_list[[n-1]] <- ccf
}

names(ccf_neurite_length_list)<- c("Tip_actin", "Tip_Kif5C", "Neurite_actin", "Neurite_Kif5C", "density_actin", "density_Kif5C")
```

```{r}
selected_ccf <- ccf_neurite_length_list$density_Kif5C  %>% as.data.table()
selected_ccf$lag<- selected_ccf$lag/freq
stacked_ccf_mat <- selected_ccf %>% gather(Neurite, ccf, -lag) # select which CCF of measurements to show
df_summary <- stacked_ccf_mat %>% group_by(lag) %>% rstatix::get_summary_stats(ccf)
```

```{r  message = FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10}
ggplot(df_summary, aes(x=lag, y=mean)) +

  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=mean-sd, ymax=mean+sd), fill="lightgrey", alpha=1)+
  geom_line(size=1) +
  
  geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color = "black")+
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color = "black")+
  
  stat_peaks(colour = "red", ignore_threshold = 0.9) +
  stat_peaks(geom = "text", colour = "red", vjust = -0.5, ignore_threshold = 0.9) +
  
  stat_valleys(colour = "blue", ignore_threshold = 0.9, span = 11)+
  stat_valleys(geom = "text", colour = "blue", angle = 45, vjust = 1.5, hjust = 1, ignore_threshold = 0.9, span = 11)+
  
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(-0.4, 0.7), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 7))+
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(-50, 50), breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 8)) + 
  
  theme_ipsum(grid = F, ticks = T)+ 
  theme_linedraw()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.text=element_text(size=12, face="bold")) #for centering the text
```
```{r}
ggsave(
  "length_neurite_Kif5C.pdf",
  plot = last_plot(),
  device = "pdf")
```

# Here we separate the outrgwoth and retraction events first, sum the changes of all neurites, and then do smoothening. (separate -> sum -> smoothening)
```{r}
all_sta_PN_list <- list()

for (k in 1:length(all_sta_list)){
  
  selected_list <- all_sta_list[[k]]
  sta_PN_list <- list()
  
  thres= 0.0 # change threshold here
  
  for (m in 1:length(selected_list)) {
    
    DT <- selected_list[[m]] %>% as.data.frame()
   
    DT_pos<- DT
    DT_neg<- DT
  
    for (n in 1:dim(DT)[2]){
        DT_pos[, n][DT_pos[, n] < 0] <- 0 
        DT_neg[, n][DT_neg[, n] > 0] <- 0 
    }
    
    mat_sum <- data.frame(rowSums(DT_pos), -rowSums(DT_neg)) # add neg sign
    
   # mat_sum [, 1][mat_sum [, 1] < thres] <- 0 
   # mat_sum [, 2][mat_sum [, 2] > -thres] <- 0
    
    #mat_sum <- mat_sum %>% forecast::ma(5)                    # smoothing #2
  
    colnames(mat_sum)<- c(paste("Pos_", names(all_list)[k], sep = ""), paste("Neg_", names(all_list)[k], sep = ""))
    sta_PN_list[[m]] <- mat_sum
  
  }
  
  names(sta_PN_list)<-  names(selected_list)
  
  all_sta_PN_list[[k]]<- sta_PN_list
  
}

names(all_sta_PN_list)<- names(all_list )
```

# Combine all stationary PN data of each videos, and add stationary soma data
```{r}
comb_sta_list<- list()

k <- all_sta_PN_list[[1]]%>% length()

for (m in 1:k){
  
  mat <- all_sta_PN_list[[1]][m] %>% as.data.frame()
  colnames(mat)<- sub('.*\\.', '', colnames(mat))
  
  for (n in 2:length(all_sta_PN_list)){
    
    mat2 <- all_sta_PN_list[[n]][m] %>% as.data.table()
    colnames(mat2)<- sub('.*\\.', '', colnames(mat2))
    
    mat <- data.frame(mat, mat2)
  }
  
  Dim <- mat %>% nrow()
  NM <- all_sta_PN_list[[1]][m] %>% names()
  mat3 <- mts_stationary_list[[NM]][1:Dim, c(2, 3)] %>% as.data.table() # soma data
  mat <- data.frame(mat, mat3)
  
 comb_sta_list[[m]]<- mat %>% ts(frequency = freq, start = c(0, 1)) 

}

names(comb_sta_list)<-   all_sta_PN_list[[1]] %>% names()
```

# cross-correlation of stationary PN data

```{r warning=FALSE}
selected_list <- comb_sta_list
ccf_PN_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = length(all_list)+1)

names(ccf_PN_list)<- c(names(all_list), "soma")

for (n in 1:8){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (selected_list[[1]][,(n*2-1)],  
                            selected_list[[1]][,(n*2)], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (selected_list[[m]][,(n*2-1)],  
                            selected_list[[m]][,(n*2)], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
                            
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_PN_list[[n]] <- ccf
}

```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# cross-correlation with stationary Pos_length data

ccf_Poslen_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = dim(selected_list[[1]])[2]-2)

names(ccf_Poslen_list)<- colnames(selected_list[[1]])[-c(1,2)] 

for (n in 3:16){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (selected_list[[1]][,c("Pos_length")],  
                            selected_list[[1]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (selected_list[[m]][,c("Pos_length")],  
                            selected_list[[m]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
  
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_Poslen_list[[n-2]] <- ccf
}
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# cross-correlation with stationary Neg_length data

ccf_Neglen_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = dim(selected_list[[1]])[2]-2)

names(ccf_Neglen_list)<- colnames(selected_list[[1]])[-c(1,2)] 

for (n in 3:16){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (selected_list[[1]][,c("Neg_length")],  
                            selected_list[[1]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (selected_list[[m]][,c("Neg_length")],  
                            selected_list[[m]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=50) 
  
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_Neglen_list[[n-2]] <- ccf
}
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# cross-correlation of actin-Kif5C data

ccf_ActKif5C_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = 7)

names(ccf_ActKif5C_list)<- c(paste("Pos_", c("tip", "neurite", "Density"), sep=""), paste("Neg_", c("tip", "neurite", "Density"), sep=""), "soma")

for (n in 1:7){
  
  pair_df<- selected_list[[1]][,grep(names(ccf_ActKif5C_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[1]]))] 
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (pair_df[ ,1],  
                      pair_df[ ,2], 
                      type = "correlation", 
                      na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                      lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
    
     pair_df2<- selected_list[[m]][,grep(names(ccf_ActKif5C_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[m]]))] 
  
      ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (pair_df2[ ,1],  
                          pair_df2[ ,2], 
                          type = "correlation", 
                          na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                          lag.max=50) 
      
      ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
      
      ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_ActKif5C_list[[n]] <- ccf
}
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# cross-correlation with soma actin

ccf_Actsoma_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = 8)

names(ccf_Actsoma_list)<- c(paste("Pos_", c("length", "tip_actin", "neurite_actin", "Density_actin"), sep=""), paste("Neg_", c("length","tip_actin", "neurite_actin", "Density_actin"), sep=""))

for (n in 1:8){
  
  df1 <- selected_list[[1]][ ,"soma_actin"]
  df2 <- selected_list[[1]][,grep(names(ccf_Actsoma_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[n]]))] 
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (df1,  
                      df2, 
                      type = "correlation", 
                      na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                      lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
     df1 <- selected_list[[m]][ ,"soma_actin"]
     df2 <- selected_list[[m]][,grep(names(ccf_Actsoma_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[n]]))]  
  
      ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (df1,  
                          df2, 
                          type = "correlation", 
                          na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                          lag.max=50) 
      
      ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
      
      ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_Actsoma_list[[n]] <- ccf
}
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
# cross-correlation with soma Kif5C

ccf_Kif5Csoma_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = 8)

names(ccf_Kif5Csoma_list)<- c(paste("Pos_", c("length", "tip_Kif5C", "neurite_Kif5C", "Density_Kif5C"), sep=""), paste("Neg_", c("length","tip_Kif5C", "neurite_Kif5C", "Density_Kif5C"), sep=""))

for (n in 1:8){
  
  df1 <- selected_list[[1]][ ,"soma_Kif5C"]
  df2 <- selected_list[[1]][,grep(names(ccf_Kif5Csoma_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[n]]))] 
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf (df1,  
                      df2, 
                      type = "correlation", 
                      na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                      lag.max=50) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
     df1 <- selected_list[[m]][ ,"soma_Kif5C"]
     df2 <- selected_list[[m]][,grep(names(ccf_Kif5Csoma_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[n]]))]  
  
      ccfPlot_2 <- ccf (df1,  
                          df2, 
                          type = "correlation", 
                          na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                          lag.max=50) 
      
      ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
      
      ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-50:50)
  ccf_Kif5Csoma_list[[n]] <- ccf
}

ccf_all_list<-list(PN=ccf_PN_list, Poslen=ccf_Poslen_list, Neglen=ccf_Neglen_list, ActKif5C=ccf_ActKif5C_list, somaActin=ccf_Actsoma_list, somaKif5C=ccf_Kif5Csoma_list)

```

```{r}
selected_ccf <- ccf_all_list$Poslen$soma_Kif5C %>% as.data.table()

selected_ccf$lag<- selected_ccf$lag/freq

stacked_ccf_mat <- selected_ccf %>% gather(Neurite, ccf, -lag) # select which CCF of measurements to show
df_summary <- stacked_ccf_mat %>% group_by(lag) %>% rstatix::get_summary_stats(ccf)
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  "avg_2.eps",
  plot = last_plot(),
  device = "eps")
```
# Soma actin and soma Kif5C
```{r}

subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
soma_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("soma", subfolder_list) ]

somaActin_list<- list()
somaKif5C_list<- list()

freq <- 1 # set frequency (1/interval)

# read csv files in the subfolders of subfolder list
for (n in 1:length(soma_list))  
{
  csv_list <- list.files(soma_list[n],pattern = "^soma_Actin(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  somaActin_list[[n]]  <- csv_list[1] %>% read.csv(header = TRUE) %>% as.data.table()
  
  csv_list <- list.files(soma_list[n],pattern = "^soma_Kif5C(.*)csv$", 
                     recursive = F,  #list of subfolders and files within the subfolders
                     include.dirs = F, # Should subdirectory names be included 
                     full.name = TRUE)  #get the full name (path and file name) of each file
  
  somaKif5C_list[[n]]  <- csv_list[1] %>% read.csv(header = TRUE) %>% as.data.table()

}

soma_actin <- do.call("cbind", somaActin_list)
soma_Kif5C <- do.call("cbind", somaKif5C_list)
```

# Create time-series data of total neurite length, Kif5C and actin intensity/density at neurite tip, shaft, and soma. 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
mts_list<- list()
mts_avg_list <- list()
mts_stationary_list <- list()

for (n in 1:length(length_list))  {
 
  mat <- length_list[[n]] %>% rowSums() %>% as.data.table() # total neurite length
  colnames(mat) <- "sum_length"
  
  # get soma intensity
  ColName <- names(length_list)[n]
  
  Dim <- nrow(Tip_Kif5C_list[[n]])
  
  soma_actin_1<- soma_actin[1:Dim, ] %>% select(all_of(ColName)) %>%  as.matrix() %>% pracma::hampel(k=5)
  mat$soma_actin <-  soma_actin_1$y
  
  soma_Kif5C_1<- soma_Kif5C[1:Dim, ] %>% select(all_of(ColName)) %>%  as.matrix() %>% pracma::hampel(k=5)
  mat$soma_Kif5C <- soma_Kif5C_1$y
  
  mat$sum_tip_actin <- Tip_actin_list[[n]] %>% rowSums()
  mat$sum_tip_Kif5C <- Tip_Kif5C_list[[n]] %>% rowSums() 
  
  mat$sum_neurite_actin <-  Neurite_actin_list[[n]] %>% rowSums() # total actin intensity at the whole neurite
  mat$sum_neurite_Kif5C <-  Neurite_Kif5C_list[[n]] %>% rowSums() # total Kif5C intensity at the whole neurite
  
  mat$sum_actin <- (Neurite_actin_list[[n]]- Tip_actin_list[[n]]) %>% rowSums() 
  mat$sum_Kif5C <- (Neurite_Kif5C_list[[n]]- Tip_Kif5C_list[[n]]) %>% rowSums()  
  
  mat$sum_actin_density <- mat$sum_neurite_actin/mat$sum_length # actin density at the whole neurite
  mat$sum_Kif5C_density <- mat$sum_neurite_Kif5C/mat$sum_length # Kif5C density at the whole neurite

  mts_list[[n]]<- mat 
  
}

names(mts_list)<- names(Tip_Kif5C_list)

```

```{r}

selected_list <- mts_list
mts_stationary_list <- list()

  for (n in 1:length(selected_list))  {
 
  mts_stationary_list[[n]]<- selected_list[[n]] %>% 
    forecast::ma(5) %>%                                    # smoothing #1
    MTS::diffM(d=1) %>% 
    forecast::ma(5) %>%                                    # smoothing #2                                   
    ts(frequency = freq, start = c(0, 1))
  
  colnames(mts_stationary_list[[n]])<-colnames(selected_list[[n]])
  
  }

names(mts_stationary_list)<- names(selected_list)

```

# cross-correlation with sum neurite length
```{r warning=FALSE}

selected_list <- mts_stationary_list

ccf_sum_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = ncol(selected_list[[1]])-1)

names(ccf_sum_list)<- colnames(selected_list[[1]])[-1]

for (n in 2:11){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf(selected_list[[1]][,1],  
                            selected_list[[1]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=80) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf(selected_list[[m]][,1],  
                            selected_list[[m]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=80) 
  
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-80:80)
  ccf_sum_list[[n-1]] <- ccf
}

```

# cross-correlation of actin-Kif5C data
```{r warning=FALSE}

selected_list <-mts_stationary_list

ccf_sumActKif5C_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = 4)

names(ccf_sumActKif5C_list)<- c("soma", "sum_tip", "sum_neurite", "density")

for (n in 1:4){
  
  pair_df<- selected_list[[1]][,grep(names(ccf_sumActKif5C_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[1]]))] 
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf(pair_df[ ,1],  
                      pair_df[ ,2], 
                      type = "correlation", 
                      na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                      lag.max=80) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
    
     pair_df2<- selected_list[[m]][,grep(names(ccf_sumActKif5C_list)[n],  colnames(selected_list[[m]]))] 
  
      ccfPlot_2 <- ccf(pair_df2[ ,1],  
                          pair_df2[ ,2], 
                          type = "correlation", 
                          na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                          lag.max=80) 
      
      ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
      
      ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-80:80)
  ccf_sumActKif5C_list[[n]] <- ccf
}

```

# cross-correlation with soma actin
```{r warning=FALSE}

selected_list <-mts_stationary_list

ccf_sumActsoma_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = ncol(selected_list[[1]]))

names(ccf_sumActsoma_list)<- colnames(selected_list[[1]])

for (n in 1:11){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf(selected_list[[1]][,2],  
                            selected_list[[1]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=80) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf(selected_list[[m]][,2],  
                            selected_list[[m]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=80) 
  
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-80:80)
  ccf_sumActsoma_list[[n]] <- ccf
}
```

# cross-correlation with soma Kif5C
```{r warning=FALSE}

selected_list <- mts_stationary_list

ccf_sumKif5Csoma_list<- vector(mode = "list", length = ncol(selected_list[[1]]))

names(ccf_sumKif5Csoma_list)<- colnames(selected_list[[1]])

for (n in 1:11){
  
    ccfPlot <- ccf(selected_list[[1]][,3],  
                            selected_list[[1]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=80) 
    
    ccf <- ccfPlot[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  for (m in 2:length(selected_list)) {
  
  ccfPlot_2 <- ccf(selected_list[[m]][,3],  
                            selected_list[[m]][,n], 
                            type = "correlation", 
                            na.action = na.pass,  plot=F, 
                            lag.max=80) 
  
  ccf_2 <- ccfPlot_2[["acf"]] %>% as.data.frame()
  
  ccf <- cbind(ccf, ccf_2)
  
  }
    qqq
  colnames(ccf)<- names(selected_list)
  ccf$lag<- c(-80:80)
  ccf_sumKif5Csoma_list[[n]] <- ccf
}
```

```{r}
ccf_sum_all_list<-list(sumlength=ccf_sum_list, ActKif5C= ccf_sumActKif5C_list, somaActin=ccf_sumActsoma_list, somaKif5C=ccf_sumKif5Csoma_list)
```

```{r}
selected_ccf <- ccf_sum_all_list$sumlength$sum_actin_density %>% as.data.table()

selected_ccf$lag<- selected_ccf$lag/freq

stacked_ccf_mat <- selected_ccf %>% gather(Neurite, ccf, -lag) 
df_summary <- stacked_ccf_mat %>% group_by(lag) %>% rstatix::get_summary_stats(ccf, type = "common")
```

# Significance of the correlation function
The variance of the cross-correlation coefficient under the null hypothesis of zero correlation is approx 1/n where n is the length of the series. The coefficients are also asymptotically normal. So approximate critical values (at the 5% level) are ±2/√n

To reduce the effect of noise, the correlation functions for all sampling windows were averaged by a spline fit (Fig. S4b). To test whether variable relationships also contributed significantly to the window-to-window variation of the correlation function we calculated confidence intervals for the average correlation function of a particular cell in two ways: i) The 95% confidence  level  of  Pearson’s  correlation  coefficient  is  defined  by  1.96N,  with  N  denoting  the  number of samples in the time course (blue lines in Fig. S4b). ii) Variation of the correlation functions about  the  spline  fit  was  calculated  from  bootstrapping  2000  samples  from  the  residuals  to  the  spline  fit8.  

The   correlation   function   for   a   cell   was   considered   insignificant   if   either   the   correlation   maximum/minimum  did  not  reach  above/below  the  95%  confidence  levels  of  Pearson’s  correlation  coefficient,  or  the  bootstrapped  95%  confidence  interval  about  the  fitted  spline  included  0  at  the  position    of    the    maximum/minimum.    Cells    without    as    significant    correlation    coefficient    maximum/minimum were eliminated from further analysis. 
```{r  message = FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10}
ggplot(df_summary, aes(x=lag, y=mean)) +
   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=mean-sd, ymax=mean+sd), fill="lightgrey")+
   geom_line(size=1, alpha=1) +
  
   stat_peaks(colour = "red", span = 15, ignore_threshold = 0.5) +
  stat_peaks(geom = "text", colour = "red", span = 15, ignore_threshold = 0.6, hjust = 2, angle = 0) +
  stat_valleys(colour = "lightblue", span = 13, ignore_threshold = 0.9) +
 stat_valleys(geom = "text", colour = "blue", span = 13, ignore_threshold = 0.9, hjust = -0.5, angle = 0) +
  
   theme_ipsum(grid = F, ticks = T)+ 
   geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color = "black")+
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color = "black")+
   scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(-0.8 ,0.5),  breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 6))+
   scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),  breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 8), limits = c(-50,50)) +
  
  theme_linedraw()+
   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        axis.text=element_text(size=12, face="bold")) #for centering the text
```

```{r}
ggsave(
  "sumLength_NT_Kif5C.pdf",
  plot = last_plot(),
  device = "pdf")
```

```{r  message = FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=10}
fig_A <- stacked_ccf_mat %>% 
ggplot(aes(x=lag, y=ccf)) + geom_point(alpha=0.3, size=0.5) +
   geom_line(aes(color=Cell), size=0.8)+ theme(legend.position = "none") 

fig_B <-stacked_ccf_mat %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = lag, y = ccf, fill=Cell)) +
    geom_area(alpha=0.5 , size=.5, colour="grey") +
    scale_fill_viridis(discrete = T) +  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    theme_ipsum(grid = F, ticks = T)+ theme(legend.position = "none") 

fig_C <-ggplot(df_summary, aes(x=Time, y=mean)) +
   geom_line(size=1, alpha=0.8) +
   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin=mean-sd, ymax=mean+sd), fill="blue", alpha=0.2)

ggpubr::ggarrange(fig_A,fig_B,fig_C,
                  labels = LETTERS[1:3],
                  ncol = 2, nrow = 2,  legend="none")

```

# Time-lagged cross-corrleation of outgrowth and retraction profiles
```{r warning=FALSE}
# constructing partial dependence (i.e., marginal effect) plots 
library(pdp)
theme_set(theme_bw())
mts_avg_list[[2]][ ,c("Length.2", "Length.4")] %>%  autoplot() +
  ylim(0, 45) +
  ggtitle (plot_title) +
  geom_line(aes(),size=1)  +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
        plot.margin = margin(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, "cm"),panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text=element_text(size=12, face="bold")) #for centering the text
```

```{r}
Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
saved_path <- paste(Path_1, "df_list.RData",  sep = "/")
save.image(file=saved_path)
```

```{r}
# Load required packages
library(biwavelet)
library(viridis)
library(ggplot2)

# Generate time series data
# Set the number of data points
n <- 100

# Generate time steps
time <- seq(0, 10, length.out = n)

# Generate random values
values <- rnorm(n)

# Combine time and values into a matrix
x <- cbind(time, values)

# Set wavelet parameters
dj <- 0.25
s0 <- 2*dt
J <- floor(log2(n*dt/s0)/dj)

# Compute wavelet spectrum
wavelet_spec <- wt(x, dj = dj, s0 = s0, J = J)
dt <- wavelet_spec$power %>% reshape2::melt()
```

```{r}
# Plot wavelet spectrum
ggplot(dt, aes(x = Var2, y = Var1)) + 
  geom_raster(aes(fill=value)) + 
 # scale_fill_gradientn(colours = pal(100), name = "Power") +
  scale_fill_viridis()+
  labs(x="Time", y="Period", title="Matrix") +
  theme_bw() + theme(axis.text.x=element_text(size=9, angle=0, vjust=0.3),
                     axis.text.y=element_text(size=9),
                     plot.title=element_text(size=11))

x <-x  %>% as.data.frame()
ggplot(x, aes(x= time, y=values)) +
  geom_line()
```

# Simulation of probability distribution
Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

## Package installation
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(jtools)
jtools::summ
jtools::plot_summs
effect_plot

# Regression imputation fits a statistical model on a variable with missing values. Predictions of this regression model are used to substitute the missing values in this variable.
# https://www.analyticsvidhya.com/blog/2016/03/tutorial-powerful-packages-imputing-missing-values/
library(VIM)
VIM::regressionImp 

library(mice) #Multivariate Imputation by Chained Equations. https://data.library.virginia.edu/getting-started-with-multiple-imputation-in-r/
#https://www.rpubs.com/anilpmm/regression_det_stoc
#https://statisticsglobe.com/regression-imputation-stochastic-vs-deterministic/

# Deterministic regression imputation
imp <- mice(data, method = "norm.predict", m = 1) %>% complete() # Impute data

# Stochastic regression imputation
imp <- mice(data, method = "norm.nob", m = 1) # Impute data

#linear mixed models
library(lme4)
#https://ourcodingclub.github.io/tutorials/mixed-models/
#https://ase.tufts.edu/bugs/guide/assets/mixed_model_guide.html
lme4::lmer

library("metaSEM")
metaSEM::reml

#Min-Max algorithms for Variance Component Mixed Model Inference.
MM4LMM::MMEst

#REML is a method for estimating variance components in models with random effects. If all effects are fixed, then using REML makes no sense because the first thing REML does, computationally speaking, is removing all fixed effects and evaluating remaining variance that belongs to random effects. Won't work very well if there are no random effects to be evaluated.
Hmisc::rm.boot() #Bootstrap Repeated Measurements Model
quantreg::rq
```
Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
